page: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 architecture virtual server belonging to the High Frequency Compute family. It features 6 shared Intel Skylake virtual cores operating at 2.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 448 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across several standard compute metrics. The stress-ng single-core score is 1670.29, and the multi-core score is 9438.87, both falling within the middle 50% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is a relative strength, scoring in the top 25% at 33.56 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth exhibits notable bottlenecks; while small-block cached reads are average at 674.29 GB/sec, large-block uncached reads (26.11 GB/sec) and writes (13.1 GB/sec) fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and weak large-block memory bandwidth, this server exhibits performance limitations under heavy memory-bound or highly parallelized workloads, as seen in its lower-tier multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database operation scores. It is also poorly suited for medium-sized LLM inference, where text generation drops to 4.88 tokens/sec. This configuration is best suited for general-purpose workloads, light web serving, single-threaded file processing, and development environments that benefit from local NVMe storage without requiring dedicated CPU resources.
description: The Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 6 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 24.0 GB of RAM. It includes a local 448 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks place this server in the middle tier for general compute, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 9438.87 and a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 33.56 GB/sec. However, it exhibits significant bottlenecks in large-block memory write bandwidth (13.1 GB/sec) and database operations. Given the shared CPU allocation and memory throughput constraints, this server is best suited for general-purpose applications, basic web serving, and development environments rather than memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 server with 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and a 448 GB NVMe SSD. It offers balanced general-purpose performance with some memory bandwidth limitations.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 server featuring 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 448 GB NVMe storage, suitable for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining moderate x86 compute, 24 GB of RAM, and local NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 6 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
24.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Bundled 448 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Reduced performance in large-block memory writes and database operations
Best suited for general-purpose applications and development environments
